chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Pett. No.S-329 of 2025 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of main case 05.12.2025 Miss. Khurshed Sahito, Advocate a/w petitioners Agha Athar Hussain, Assistant Advocate General Mr. Khalil Ahmed Maitlo, Deputy Prosecutor General a/w Insp. Mehtab Ali Khawar SHO PS Mashakh Hoti, District Tando Allahyar, ASI Rehmtullah Chandio, PS Shahdadpur, District Sanghar, Insp. Mashooque Ali Jamro on behalf of DSP Gambat, SIP Ali Murad Narejo, on behalf of SSP Khairpur, SIP Iran Khan Ujjan on behalf of SHO PS Ranipur ************** The petitioner No.1 submits that she, being sui juris, has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2 of her own free will, consent, and without any coercion or undue influence. Both petitioners submit that they apprehend harassment at the hands of the private respondents and have, therefore, sought protection of their fundamental rights enshrined under Articles 4, 9, and 14 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. 2. Mr. Illahi Bux Jamali, Advocate, has filed vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents No. 9, 15, and 17, which is taken on record. 3. The official respondents have also filed their statements, which are taken on record. It is stated therein that they will act strictly in accordance with law and shall not cause any harassment to the petitioners. It is further undertaken that, in case any complaint is received from the petitioners regarding harassment or unlawful interference, the same shall be dealt with strictly in accordance with law. 4. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that, in view of the statement filed on behalf of the official respondents, learned counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of instant petition.

It is further undertaken that, in case any complaint is received from the petitioners regarding harassment or unlawful interference, the same shall be dealt with strictly in accordance with law. 4. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that, in view of the statement filed on behalf of the official respondents, learned counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of instant petition. Accordingly, this Constitutional Petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to strictly adhere to their undertaking and to ensure that no harassment or unlawful action is caused to the petitioners. If any cognizable offence is reported, the same shall be proceeded with strictly in accordance with law. Since the petitioners have expressed apprehension, therefore officials present in Court are directed to provide protection to the petitioners until they reach their destination. J U D G E M.Ali*
